subtopics.title: Types of Analytics – Descriptive, Diagnostic, Predictive, and Prescriptive

blocks.title: Types of Analytics
blocks.subtitle: From Understanding the Past to Shaping the Future

blocks.title: Descriptive Analytics – What Happened?
blocks.content: Descriptive analytics answers the question 'What happened?' It summarizes historical data to identify patterns and trends. It uses techniques like reporting, data aggregation, and data visualization. Examples: Dashboards, sales reports, website traffic analytics. It forms the foundation for all other analytics.

blocks.title: Diagnostic Analytics – Why Did It Happen?
blocks.content: Diagnostic analytics answers 'Why did it happen?' It goes beyond describing what happened to understand the root causes. Techniques: Drill-down, data mining, correlation analysis, and hypothesis testing. Examples: Determining why sales dropped in a region, why website traffic declined, why customer churn increased.

blocks.title: Predictive Analytics – What Will Happen?
blocks.content: Predictive analytics answers 'What will happen?' It uses historical data and machine learning to forecast future outcomes. Techniques: Regression analysis, time series forecasting, machine learning models (classification, regression). Examples: Sales forecasting, customer churn prediction, demand forecasting.

blocks.title: Prescriptive Analytics – What Should We Do?
blocks.content: Prescriptive analytics answers 'What should we do?' It recommends actions based on predictions and optimization. Techniques: Optimization algorithms, simulation, decision trees. Examples: Recommending pricing strategies, inventory optimization, resource allocation, personalized recommendations.

blocks.title: Example: Analytics in E-Commerce
given: An e-commerce company uses all four types of analytics.
blocks.find: Explain how each type is used.
steps: Descriptive: The dashboard shows total sales, top products, and traffic trends.
Diagnostic: Analysis reveals that a drop in sales was due to a competitor's price drop.
Predictive: ML models forecast next month's sales and predict customer churn.
Prescriptive: The system recommends dynamic pricing to remain competitive.
blocks.answer: All four analytics types work together to drive e-commerce decision-making.

blocks.title: Trap: Predictive and prescriptive analytics are the same.
blocks.wrong: Predictive and prescriptive analytics are identical.
blocks.correct: Predictive forecasts what will happen; prescriptive recommends what to do about it.

blocks.title: Key Points – Types of Analytics
points: Diagnostic: Why did it happen? (Root cause)
The four types form a progression from understanding to action.

subtopics.title: Analytical Techniques – Statistical Analysis, Predictive Modeling, Trend Analysis, Forecasting, and Data Mining

blocks.title: Analytical Techniques
blocks.subtitle: Tools to Extract Insights from Data

blocks.title: Statistical Analysis – Understanding Data Distributions
blocks.content: Statistical analysis involves collecting, organizing, and interpreting data. It is the foundation of analytics. Key concepts: Mean, median, mode, variance, standard deviation, correlation, and hypothesis testing. It helps describe data and identify relationships.

blocks.title: Predictive Modeling – Using ML for Predictions
blocks.content: Predictive modeling uses machine learning algorithms to predict future outcomes based on historical data. Common models: Linear regression, logistic regression, decision trees, random forest, SVM, neural networks. It is used for classification, regression, and time series forecasting.

blocks.title: Trend Analysis – Identifying Patterns Over Time
blocks.content: Trend analysis examines data over time to identify long-term movements. It uses techniques like moving averages, exponential smoothing, and regression. Applications: Sales trends, market trends, user behavior trends.

blocks.title: Forecasting – Predicting Future Values
blocks.content: Forecasting predicts future values based on historical data. Techniques: Time series analysis, ARIMA models, exponential smoothing, machine learning. Applications: Sales forecasting, inventory planning, resource allocation, weather prediction.

blocks.title: Data Mining – Discovering Hidden Patterns
blocks.content: Data mining is the process of discovering patterns and knowledge from large datasets. It combines statistics, machine learning, and database systems. Techniques: Association rule mining (market basket analysis), clustering (customer segmentation), anomaly detection, classification.

blocks.title: Example: Data Mining in Retail
given: A retail chain uses data mining to improve sales.
steps: Association rule mining discovers that customers who buy diapers also buy baby wipes.
The store places these items together to increase sales.
Clustering segments customers into groups based on purchasing behavior.
Targeted marketing campaigns are created for each segment.
blocks.answer: Data mining helps retailers discover patterns, segment customers, and detect fraud.

blocks.title: Trap: Data mining and predictive modeling are the same.
blocks.wrong: Data mining and predictive modeling are identical.
blocks.correct: Data mining discovers patterns; predictive modeling uses those patterns to make predictions.
blocks.tip: Data mining = Discovery; Predictive modeling = Prediction.

blocks.title: Key Points – Analytical Techniques
points: Statistical Analysis: Describes data distributions and relationships.
Predictive Modeling: Uses ML to forecast outcomes.
Data Mining: Discovers hidden patterns (clustering, association, anomaly).

blocks.title: Exam Strategy
blocks.content: Focus on the four types of analytics (Descriptive, Diagnostic, Predictive, Prescriptive) and key analytical techniques (statistical analysis, predictive modeling, trend analysis, forecasting, data mining).

blocks.question: Which type of analytics answers the question 'What happened?'
blocks.explanation: Descriptive analytics summarizes historical data to answer 'What happened?'

blocks.question: Which type of analytics answers the question 'Why did it happen?'
blocks.explanation: Diagnostic analytics looks at root causes to answer 'Why did it happen?'

blocks.question: Which type of analytics answers the question 'What will happen?'
blocks.explanation: Predictive analytics uses ML and forecasting to predict future outcomes.

blocks.question: Which type of analytics answers the question 'What should we do?'
blocks.explanation: Prescriptive analytics recommends actions based on predictions and optimization.

blocks.question: Which analytical technique identifies long-term patterns in time series data?
blocks.explanation: Trend analysis identifies long-term patterns and movements over time.

blocks.question: Which analytical technique discovers hidden patterns in large datasets?
blocks.explanation: Data mining discovers hidden patterns using clustering, association, and anomaly detection.

blocks.question: Which technique is used for customer segmentation?
blocks.explanation: Clustering groups similar data points, making it ideal for customer segmentation.

blocks.question: Which technique is used for market basket analysis?
blocks.explanation: Association rule mining discovers items frequently bought together (market basket analysis).

blocks.question: Which of the following is a time series forecasting technique?
blocks.explanation: ARIMA (AutoRegressive Integrated Moving Average) is a time series forecasting technique.

blocks.question: Which statistical measure describes the spread of data?
blocks.explanation: Standard deviation measures the spread or dispersion of data around the mean.

blocks.title: Post-Test Review
blocks.content: Review the four types of analytics (Descriptive, Diagnostic, Predictive, Prescriptive) and key techniques (statistical analysis, predictive modeling, trend analysis, forecasting, data mining).
points: Techniques: Statistical, predictive modeling, trend, forecasting, data mining.
